mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer spending that translates into current-quarter revenue surprises for large-cap consumer-facing firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS growth is positive but subsequent twenty-day S&P 500 returns are negative in more than fifty percent of positive-growth months over any five-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the unemployment rate exceeding zero point three percentage points over a rolling three-month window signals a leftward shift in labor demand that precedes broad-based earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive sixty-day S&P 500 returns in more than forty-five percent of instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over any ten-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in industrial production and rise in unemployment captures a recessionary co-movement that equity markets systematically underprice relative to single-indicator benchmarks; this joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction term fails to produce a negative Rank IC exceeding zero point zero four over any three-year rolling window, or the signal underperforms the weaker single-series predictor in pairwise horserace tests.

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive monetary policy regimes, an accelerating CPI inflation rate signals increased probability of additional Fed tightening and input-cost margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: In FEDFUNDS above four point five percent regimes, positive CPIAUCSL momentum fails to predict negative sixty-day returns with a hit ratio above fifty-five percent over any five-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate raise the risk-free discount rate applied to near-term equity cash flows, causing immediate negative S&P 500 price pressure over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive five-day S&P 500 returns in more than fifty percent of months over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth below one percent annualized signals an economy operating near stall speed, raising recession probability and triggering risk-off repositioning in equities over the subsequent sixty trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth below one percent quarters are followed by positive sixty-day S&P 500 returns in more than fifty percent of cases over any ten-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in housing starts foreshadow construction-sector layoffs and tightening mortgage credit conditions, transmitting negative wealth effects to the broader economy and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month HOUST declines are followed by positive twenty-day S&P 500 returns in more than fifty percent of instances over any five-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below three percent, a concurrent month-over-month rise in unemployment signals that monetary easing is insufficient to offset accelerating labor market deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: In FEDFUNDS below three percent regimes, month-over-month UNRATE increases fail to produce negative twenty-day returns with a hit ratio above fifty-five percent over any five-year window.
